title: qPCR for Halal Verification of Dairy and Processed Ingredients
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Halal   integrity   in   dairy     and     processed     ingredients     is     a     growing     concern   as global   trade     increases     product     complexity  .   Conventional     methods     such   as ELISA   or     microscopy     may   fail   to     detect     low  -level   contamination     or     processed     materials     containing   non-halal   components  .   Quantitative   PCR (  qPCR  )   offers     a     rapid  ,   sensitive  ,   and     specific     solution     for     detecting     prohibited   DNA   sources     such   as   porcine     or   non-  slaughtered     bovine     materials  . The   technique     allows     accurate     species     identification     even   in   highly     processed     products  ,   enabling     reliable   halal   verification  .   Implementing     qPCR     within   halal   certification     frameworks     enhances     consumer     trust     and     prevents     fraudulent     labeling  .   This     approach     strengthens     regulatory     oversight  ,   supports     international     trade     compliance  ,   and     safeguards     the     economic     value     of   halal-  certified     products   in global   markets  .
